codeReview.summary: 4 examples added for L10-S4 through L10-S7: process-context-demo (l10-s4), context-switch-demo (l10-s5), virtual-memory-demo (l10-s6), page-table-demo (l10-s7). All examples have valid MASM code, annotations (4+ each), commonMistakes (3 each), and provenance. Linked correctly in lectures.json.

sharedStateObservations.observation: First worker session (d09f5a9c) failed due to missing content-worker skill in registry. Second session (97396538) succeeded by following the content-worker skill procedure correctly. This indicates the skill registry issue was transient or fixed during session.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: First handoff shows failure with 'content-worker skill is not available'; second handoff shows successful completion following skill procedure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used pcb_* naming for data variables in process-context-demo. This is a reasonable naming convention for simulated PCB structures, though no explicit naming convention exists in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: examples.json: process-context-demo uses pcb_eax, pcb_ebx, pcb_pc, etc.

summary: Feature passes scrutiny. 4 examples (process-context-demo, context-switch-demo, virtual-memory-demo, page-table-demo) added correctly with proper structure per VAL-CODE-001: non-empty code, 4 annotations each, 3 commonMistakes each, provenance.sourceFile, difficulty levels. All linked to correct section IDs (l10-s4 through l10-s7) in lectures.json, satisfying VAL-CONTENT-004. VAL-CODE-002 (2+ concepts) also satisfied: process-context-demo has 8 concepts, context-switch-demo has 7, virtual-memory-demo has 7, page-table-demo has 8. No blocking issues found.
